Long ago, before the world had a voice, the Great Mountain Oros sat in eternal silence. He was carved of obsidian and frost, a solitary sentinel watching the sky. One night, a curious star named Lyra leaned too far over the celestial balcony and plummeted toward the earth. Oros, moved by her brilliant descent, reached up to catch her. But mountain stone is unforgiving, and upon striking his peak, Lyra shattered into a million radiant fragments.

Devastated, Oros wept. His tears carved the first rivers into the valleys below. For the first time, he opened his stony mouth and cried out her name, "Lyra!"

The fragments of the star, glowing faintly in the crevices of the mountain, could not return to the heavens. Instead, to comfort the grieving giant, they caught his voice and threw it back to him. "Lyra," the stones whispered. "Lyra," they called again.

Thus, the echo was born. It was never just a trick of the wind, but the shattered pieces of a fallen star answering the lonely mountain forever. To this very day, whenever you shout loudly into a canyon, it is Lyra’s light bouncing your voice back through the vast and empty night sky.

Myth: Long before humans walked the earth, the deep canyons were guarded by Aella, a solitary stone spirit. She lived in absolute silence, her only joy coming from the nightly songs of a brilliant star named Aris. Aris sang melodies of light that cascaded down the rocky peaks, filling Aella’s lonely existence with warmth.

One twilight, Aris leaned too close to the earth and fell, shattering against the harsh obsidian cliffs. Devastated, Aella rushed to gather the radiant, broken fragments in a basket of woven silver, hoping to save his celestial voice. But the fragments were cold and silent.

To her astonishment, the shattered pieces of the star, now scattered across the canyon walls, flared with a faint glow. Wanting to comfort the grieving spirit, the fragments caught her voice and threw it back to her. "Aris," the stones whispered. 

From that day on, Aella was never truly alone. The echo was born—a perpetual, comforting dialogue between a lonely valley spirit and the fragments of a fallen star.
